highlights.text: I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “The bull case Monday is that ES can do some back-testing of the zones we broke down yesterday and today.
In an “ultra bull case” for Monday/Sunday, ES won’t even make a new low.
For Failed Breakdown traders like myself, readers know volatility just means bigger Failed Breakdowns/squeezes, and it’s what caused today’s rally after Friday’s melt-down.
I use Failed Breakdowns, which mirror institutional accumulation patterns.
It goes “elevator down” and sells violently.
It undercuts a low, recovers, puts in a Failed Breakdown, and short squeezes 2x as violently.
My edge is trading the latter, and late day Friday as readers know, we put in a quality Failed Breakdown to get us long ~6257 .
We saw a large squeeze today, but its never the initial squeeze after a rally that’s interesting, its whether bulls can then buy the dip after the first retracement of the squeeze.
All last week, key supports which I discussed were 6374 and then 6333.

post_title: Short Squeeze For SPX. Dead Cat Bounce, or Bottom? August 5th Plan
